vars.name: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
vars.comment: REQUIRED: ElevenLabs API key for voice sidecar Get your key from: https://elevenlabs.io/app/developers/api-keys Free tier includes 10,000 characters/month

vars.name: EVOKORE_ALLOW_GC_PRUNE
vars.comment: Allow "git gc --prune=now" (DC-39). When unset, explicit gc --prune=now is blocked because it removes unreachable refs immediately, which can destroy the last-resort recovery window. "git gc --auto" (implicit maintenance) is always allowed.

vars.name: EVOKORE_ANTISLOP_HOOK
vars.comment: Optional: Enable the anti-slop PostToolUse hook (warning-only). Detects wasteful tool-call patterns (re-reading after edit, 3+ consecutive reads of the same path, bash echo/printf for plain communication) and emits one stderr advisory per pattern per session. The hook always exits 0 — never blocks — so autonomous loops are preserved by construction. Disabled by default. See SKILLS/EVOKORE EXTENSIONS/anti-slop/SKILL.md for the heuristic definitions and the matching skill body.

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_EXPORT
vars.comment: Optional: Enable external audit event export (requires audit log enabled, which is the default)

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_EXPORT_BATCH_SIZE
vars.comment: Optional: Maximum number of audit entries per export batch (default: 100)

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_EXPORT_INTERVAL_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Export interval in milliseconds (default: 60000, minimum: 10000)

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_EXPORT_SECRET
vars.comment: Optional: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for audit export (optional)

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_EXPORT_URL
vars.comment: Optional: HTTP(S) URL to POST audit events to (required when export is enabled)

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUDIT_LOG
vars.comment: Optional: Structured audit logging to ~/.evokore/audit/ (enabled by default, set to false to disable) Records security and operational events as JSONL for operator review

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUTO_ACTIVATION_REQUIRE_TRUTH_SCORE
vars.comment: Block nextSteps[] auto-activation of destructive skills (tdd, pr-manager, orch-refactor, to-issues, release-readiness) until the active session has a passing verification-quality evidence row. Set to 0/false/off/no to disable. Default: on.

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUTO_FILE_ISSUES
vars.comment: Allow autonomous "gh issue create" (DC-41). When unset, autonomous issue filing is blocked so skill auto-chains (e.g., to-prd -> to-issues -> triage-bug) cannot file GitHub issues without operator confirmation. Read-only ops (gh issue list/view/comment /close) are always allowed.

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUTO_FILE_PRS
vars.comment: Allow "gh pr create" from inside an agent worktree (DC-42). When unset, gh pr create is blocked when the cwd matches */.claude/worktrees/* so throwaway worktrees and panel-of-experts agents cannot autonomously file PRs. PR creation from the repo root remains allowed (PR Template workflow).

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUTO_LABEL_ISSUES
vars.comment: Allow "gh issue|pr edit --add-label <sensitive>" (DC-43). Sensitive labels: security, release, ready-for-agent. Adding any of these labels triggers downstream automation (release pipelines, agent pickup queues, security review routing); the default-deny ensures the operator confirms intent. --remove-label is never gated.

vars.name: EVOKORE_AUTO_MEMORY_SYNC
vars.comment: Optional: Auto-sync Claude memory files when session stops (default: true) Set to false to disable automatic memory sync at session-wrap boundary

vars.name: EVOKORE_CHILD_SERVER_BOOT_TIMEOUT_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Timeout (ms) for child server boot during proxy bootstrap (default: 15000)

vars.name: EVOKORE_DASHBOARD_APPROVAL_WS_TOKEN
vars.comment: Optional: Dedicated bearer token for the approvals WebSocket endpoint. When unset, the approvals page falls back to the dashboard session token.

vars.name: EVOKORE_DASHBOARD_APPROVAL_WS_URL
vars.comment: Optional: Explicit approvals WebSocket endpoint for the dashboard approvals page. Use this when the dashboard runs on a different host/port or origin than the EVOKORE HTTP server. When unset, loopback dashboards auto-target :EVOKORE_HTTP_PORT on the current hostname; non-loopback dashboards fall back to same-origin /ws/approvals unless EVOKORE_HTTP_HOST is set.

vars.name: EVOKORE_DASHBOARD_ROLE
vars.comment: Optional: Dashboard RBAC role (default: admin for local-only, readonly for token mode) Available roles: admin, developer, readonly

vars.name: EVOKORE_DASHBOARD_TOKEN
vars.comment: Optional: Bearer token for dashboard authentication (omit for local-only mode) When set, all dashboard API routes require Authorization: Bearer <token>

vars.name: EVOKORE_DISCOVERY_PROFILE
vars.comment: Optional: Discovery profile name (default: built-in "default" — every native tool always visible). Selects a profile defined under discovery.profiles in mcp.config.json. EVOKORE_TOOL_DISCOVERY_MODE=legacy is a hard safety pin and overrides this. Five preset profiles are shipped in mcp.config.json: coding - file ops, git, testing, native code skills (<= ~8K tokens) research - read/search heavy: memory, docs, navigation (<= ~5K) voice - voice sidecar + minimal control surface (<= ~3K) legacy-full - deprecation shim: every native + proxy tool visible legacy-dynamic - v3.0 default: native always visible, proxy dynamic See docs/TOOL_DISCOVERY_PROFILES.md for measured token counts and tradeoffs.

vars.name: EVOKORE_HOME
vars.comment: Optional: Override EVOKORE runtime home directory (default: ~/.evokore). Session manifests, replay logs, evidence logs, and cache files all live under this directory. Used by src/SessionManifest.ts and the hook scripts.

vars.name: EVOKORE_HTTP_ALLOW_PRIVATE
vars.comment: Allow httpGet() to fetch from private/loopback addresses (local dev only -- NEVER enable in production)

vars.name: EVOKORE_HTTP_HOST
vars.comment: Optional: Host/interface for HTTP server mode (default: 127.0.0.1)

vars.name: EVOKORE_HTTP_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: Start EVOKORE in HTTP server mode instead of stdio (default: false)

vars.name: EVOKORE_HTTP_PORT
vars.comment: Optional: Port for HTTP server mode (default: 3100)

vars.name: EVOKORE_MCP_CONFIG_PATH
vars.comment: Optional: Override path to mcp.config.json (default: project root)

vars.name: EVOKORE_PANEL_MAX_CONCURRENCY
vars.comment: Maximum concurrent in-flight fleet_spawn operations. Spawns past the cap queue FIFO and drain on fleet_release. Clamped to [1, 50]. Default: 5.

vars.name: EVOKORE_PLUGINS_DIR
vars.comment: Optional: Custom plugins directory (default: plugins/ relative to project root)

vars.name: EVOKORE_PROTECTION_PROFILE
vars.comment: Optional: Protection profile preset that coordinates the runtime-protection knobs (TillDone Stop hook, repo-audit hook). Per-knob env vars always win. Recognized values: strict / balanced — historic blocking behavior (no overrides) autonomous — TillDone block-mode plus max_blocks=3 + idle_timeout=10min so autonomous loops cannot deadlock on stale tasks relaxed — TillDone in warn mode (allow stop, surface incomplete tasks) off — TillDone in off mode + repo-audit hook disabled Damage-control is NOT part of the profile and always stays on. Default: unset (legacy behavior, identical to the strict preset).

vars.name: EVOKORE_PROXY_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Timeout for proxied tool call requests in ms (default: 60000)

vars.name: EVOKORE_RE_GHIDRA_HEADLESS_PYTHON
vars.comment: Optional: Reverse-engineering MCP child servers (disabled by default in mcp.config.json) Provide these paths if you want EVOKORE to proxy ghidra-headless-mcp, ReVa, or binary-mcp. GHIDRA_INSTALL_DIR=C:\path\to\ghidra

vars.name: EVOKORE_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX
vars.comment: Optional: Redis key namespace prefix for session keys (default: evokore)

vars.name: EVOKORE_REDIS_URL
vars.comment: Optional: Redis connection URL for redis session store (default: redis://127.0.0.1:6379)

vars.name: EVOKORE_ROLE
vars.comment: Optional: Role-based access control (RBAC) role name (default: none = flat permissions) Available roles defined in permissions.yml: admin, developer, readonly

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_BASH_CPU_LIMIT
vars.comment: Optional: Per-language container sandbox CPU overrides. When unset, the global EVOKORE_SANDBOX_CPU_LIMIT baseline is used.

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_BASH_MEMORY_MB
vars.comment: Optional: Per-language container sandbox memory overrides in MB. When unset, the global EVOKORE_SANDBOX_MEMORY_MB baseline is used.

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_CPU_LIMIT
vars.comment: Optional: Container sandbox CPU limit (default: 1)

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_MEMORY_MB
vars.comment: Optional: Container sandbox memory limit in MB (default: 256)

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: Skill execution sandbox mode (default: auto) 'container' = always use Docker/Podman, 'process' = subprocess only, 'auto' = try container then fallback

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_PREPULL
vars.comment: Optional: Warm missing container sandbox images at startup to reduce first-run latency (default: false)

vars.name: EVOKORE_SANDBOX_SECCOMP_PROFILE
vars.comment: Optional: Custom seccomp profile JSON file for container sandbox executions. When unset, Docker/Podman runtime defaults apply.

vars.name: EVOKORE_SECURITY_DEFAULT_DENY
vars.comment: Optional: Deny unknown tools by default (secure posture � enable for production hardening) When unset or false, unknown tools default to allow (backward compatible)

vars.name: EVOKORE_SESSION_ID
vars.comment: Optional: Session identifier for the TrustLedger and worker-context env propagation.

vars.name: EVOKORE_SESSION_STORE
vars.comment: Optional: Session store backend for HTTP mode (default: file). Options: file, memory, redis

vars.name: EVOKORE_SESSION_TTL_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Session TTL in milliseconds for multi-tenant HTTP sessions (default: 3600000 = 1 hour)

vars.name: EVOKORE_SKILL_GRAPH_PATH
vars.comment: Optional: Override the path to the skill composition graph artifact (defaults to <repo-root>/skill-graph.json, generated by 'npm run skill-graph')

vars.name: EVOKORE_SKILL_WATCHER
vars.comment: Optional: Enable filesystem watcher for auto-refreshing the skill index (default: disabled)

vars.name: EVOKORE_STEERING_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: Active steering mode for ComplianceChecker (dev|research|review|debug|security-audit). Default: dev

vars.name: EVOKORE_STT_ENABLED
vars.comment: Optional: Enable STT (speech-to-text) in VoiceSidecar (default: false)

vars.name: EVOKORE_STT_LOCAL_MODEL
vars.comment: Optional: Local whisper CLI model (default: base). Options: tiny, base, small, medium, large

vars.name: EVOKORE_STT_MODEL
vars.comment: Optional: Whisper API model (default: whisper-1)

vars.name: EVOKORE_STT_PROVIDER
vars.comment: Optional: STT provider selection (default: whisper-api). Options: whisper-api, local-whisper, local

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY
vars.comment: Optional: Enable local-only usage telemetry (default: false) Collects only aggregate metrics: tool call counts, error rates, session counts, latency No PII is collected. Metrics stored at ~/.evokore/telemetry/metrics.json When EVOKORE_HTTP_MODE=true, GET /metrics exposes the same aggregate counters in Prometheus text format If EVOKORE_AUTH_REQUIRED=true, scrape /metrics with Authorization: Bearer <token>

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY_DIR
vars.comment: Optional: Override the telemetry directory (default: ~/.evokore/telemetry) Used by tests for isolation; normal operators should leave this unset.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY_EXPORT
vars.comment: Optional: Enable external telemetry export (requires EVOKORE_TELEMETRY=true)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY_EXPORT_INTERVAL_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Export interval in milliseconds (default: 60000, minimum: 10000)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY_EXPORT_SECRET
vars.comment: Optional: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for telemetry export (optional)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TELEMETRY_EXPORT_URL
vars.comment: Optional: HTTP(S) URL to POST telemetry metrics to (required when export is enabled)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TENANT_SCOPING
vars.comment: Optional: Enable tenant-scoped session directory layout (default: disabled). When set to "true", session files are namespaced under ~/.evokore/tenants/{tenantId}/sessions/ where tenantId is sourced from the OAuth JWT `sub` claim. When unset, the legacy flat layout is used.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TILLDONE_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS
vars.comment: Optional: in 'block' mode, allow stop if the tasks file has not been modified for more than this many milliseconds. Lets a stalled autonomous loop exit cleanly. Unset/zero disables this escape valve.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TILLDONE_MAX_BLOCKS
vars.comment: Optional: in 'block' mode, allow stop after N consecutive identical blocks. Counter at ~/.evokore/sessions/{sessionId}-tilldone-blocks.json resets when the tasks file is touched (the agent edited tasks). Set to a positive integer; unset/zero disables the escape valve.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TILLDONE_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: TillDone Stop-hook mode. block (default) — exit 2 when incomplete tasks remain (legacy) warn — print the warning, allow stop (exit 0) off — skip the incomplete check entirely (allow stop)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TOOL_DISCOVERY_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: Tool discovery mode. Default: dynamic (v3.1+). When unset (or set to "dynamic"), tools/list returns the always-visible native tools plus only the proxied tools the current session has activated via discover_tools. This produces a lean ~1.5-2.5K-token payload instead of the legacy 12-31K-token full proxied tool dump. Operators who depend on the pre-v3.1 behavior (every proxied tool ships in the initial tools/list) can set the safety pin below to restore it. The safety pin also overrides any EVOKORE_DISCOVERY_PROFILE selection.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TOOL_LIST_PAGINATION
vars.comment: Optional: tools/list cursor pagination. Off by default to preserve pre-v3.1 behavior. Enables automatically when the client sends a cursor param. Set EVOKORE_TOOL_LIST_PAGINATION=on to force pagination even when the first call has no cursor (useful for capped clients like Cursor IDE that silently truncate at 40 tools). Default page size 35 keeps the first page under the Cursor IDE 40-tool cap. Clamped to [1, 1000].

vars.name: EVOKORE_TOOL_SCHEMA_FALLBACK_MS
vars.comment: Optional: Compat-probe fallback window in milliseconds for deferred mode. If no client calls describe_tool within this window after the first tools/list response, the runtime reverts to full mode and emits a one-time stderr warning. Default: 60000.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TOOL_SCHEMA_MODE
vars.comment: Optional: Tool-list schema-deferred mode. Default: full. When set to "deferred", tools/list responses ship each tool with a placeholder inputSchema and _meta: { schema_deferred: true }; clients must call describe_tool to fetch full schemas. Only enable when you have personally verified the client tolerates placeholder schemas and implements the describe_tool bootstrap path.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TOOLS_LIST_CHANGED_DEBOUNCE_MS
vars.comment: f runtime guards tools/list_changed debounce window in milliseconds. Coalesces rapid notifications (discover_tools, execute_skill nextSteps activation, skill watcher refreshes). 0 disables debounce. Clamped to [0, 5000]. Default: 250.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TRUTH_SCORE_EVIDENCE_WINDOW
vars.comment: Tail-window size when scanning the active session evidence log for verification-quality rows. Bounded to [1, 1000]. Default: 50.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TRUTH_SCORE_THRESHOLD
vars.comment: Numeric truth-score threshold when no passed:true field is present. Rows with a numeric score field >= threshold satisfy the gate. Default: 0.7.

vars.name: EVOKORE_TTS_API_KEY
vars.comment: Optional: API key for OpenAI-compatible TTS endpoint (not required for local servers)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TTS_BASE_URL
vars.comment: Optional: Base URL for OpenAI-compatible TTS endpoint (default: http://127.0.0.1:8880) Used when EVOKORE_TTS_PROVIDER=openai-compat (e.g., Kokoro-FastAPI, Chatterbox TTS API)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TTS_MODEL
vars.comment: Optional: Default model for OpenAI-compatible TTS (default: tts-1)

vars.name: EVOKORE_TTS_PROVIDER
vars.comment: Optional: TTS provider for VoiceSidecar (default: elevenlabs). Options: elevenlabs, openai-compat

vars.name: EVOKORE_TTS_VOICE
vars.comment: Optional: Default voice name for OpenAI-compatible TTS (default: nova)

vars.name: EVOKORE_WEBHOOKS_ALLOW_PRIVATE
vars.comment: Allow webhooks to private/loopback addresses (local dev only � NEVER enable in production)

vars.name: EVOKORE_WEBHOOKS_ENABLED
vars.comment: Optional: Enable webhook event notifications to external systems (default: false) Configure webhook URLs in mcp.config.json under the webhooks key

vars.name: EVOKORE_WHISPER_PATH
vars.comment: Optional: Path to local whisper CLI binary (default: whisper)

vars.name: EVOKORE_WORKER_AUTODISPATCH
vars.comment: Optional: Disable purpose-gate keyword-triggered worker auto-dispatch (default: enabled).

vars.name: EVOKORE_WORKERS_ENABLED
vars.comment: Optional: Disable background worker intervals (claims janitor, DLQ). Default: true.

vars.name: EVOKORE_WS_ALLOW_QUERY_TOKEN
vars.comment: Deprecated: allow WS auth via ?token= query string (default: false, prefer Authorization header)

vars.name: EVOKORE_WS_APPROVALS_ENABLED
vars.comment: Optional: Enable WebSocket real-time approval events on HttpServer (default: false) When enabled, the /ws/approvals WebSocket endpoint streams approval lifecycle events

vars.name: EVOKORE_WS_HEARTBEAT_MS
vars.comment: Optional: WebSocket heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default: 30000)

vars.name: EVOKORE_WS_MAX_CLIENTS
vars.comment: Optional: Maximum concurrent WebSocket approval clients (default: 10)

vars.name: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
vars.comment: REQUIRED: GitHub Personal Access Token for MCP GitHub server

vars.name: STITCH_API_KEY
vars.comment: REQUIRED: Google Stitch API key for the Stitch MCP server Configure this if you enable the Stitch server in mcp.config.json

vars.name: SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN
vars.comment: REQUIRED: Supabase access token for MCP Supabase server Get your token from: https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens

vars.name: VOICE_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT_DIR
vars.comment: Optional: Directory for voice sidecar audio artifacts

vars.name: VOICE_SIDECAR_DISABLE_PLAYBACK
vars.comment: Optional: Disable voice sidecar audio playback (set to 1)

vars.name: VOICE_SIDECAR_MAX_CONNECTIONS
vars.comment: Optional: Maximum concurrent voice sidecar WebSocket clients (default: 5)

vars.name: VOICE_SIDECAR_PORT
vars.comment: Optional: Voice Sidecar port (default: 8888)
